automotive-ev-battery.title: Volvo gives customers access to Tesla's Supercharger network – in Europe
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volvo Cars will grant its electric vehicle customers access to Tesla's Supercharger network across Europe beginning in the fourth quarter of 2026, providing connectivity to over 20,000 charging stations through the Volvo app. The Swedish automaker also plans to strengthen its alignment with Tesla's charging ecosystem in the Asia-Pacific region, marking a significant industry collaboration on charging infrastructure.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Ferrari's first EV 'Luce' to launch from €550,000
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Ferrari has officially unveiled its first all-electric vehicle, the Luce, featuring four electric motors delivering 772 kW of power and a 122-kWh battery on a newly developed electric platform. Priced from €550,000, the luxury EV breaks from Ferrari tradition by offering a four-door five-seater configuration rather than a classic two-seater sports car.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Nissan may abandon plans for Sunderland e-axle factory
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Nissan subsidiary Jatco has reportedly abandoned plans to construct an electric axle manufacturing facility in Sunderland, UK, which would have been the company's first European production site for EV components. The decision reflects weak demand for Nissan electric vehicles in Europe and represents a setback for the company's electrification strategy.

automotive-ev-battery.title: DHL Supply Chain opens battery logistics hub in the Netherlands
automotive-ev-battery.summary: DHL Supply Chain has begun construction of a specialized logistics hub for high-voltage EV and battery storage system batteries in Holtum, Netherlands, with operations expected to commence in early 2027. The facility represents a significant investment in battery supply chain infrastructure to support Europe's growing electric vehicle market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Sany to deploy over 100 electric trucks in Vietnam
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese manufacturer Sany has entered Vietnam's electric heavy-duty truck market with the launch of the EV550 tractor, already securing orders for more than 100 units. The expansion demonstrates growing momentum in the commercial electric vehicle segment in Southeast Asia.

automotive-ev-battery.title: New York eases electric school bus mandate
automotive-ev-battery.summary: New York State has delayed its electric school bus procurement mandate from 2027 to 2032 and extended the deadline for complete fleet electrification by five years to 2040 in its newly presented budget. The postponement represents a significant setback to the state's electrification timeline for public transportation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Colombia EV Sales Report: Sales Grow 316% in April, EVs Now +20% Market Share
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Colombia's electric vehicle market experienced explosive growth in April 2026, with sales surging 316 percent year-over-year and battery electric vehicles achieving nearly 20 percent market share compared to 7.4 percent the previous year. The dramatic acceleration in EV adoption significantly exceeds earlier market projections for the Latin American nation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Volvo Buses secures order for 47 battery-electric buses from southern Sweden
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Swedish regional operator Bergkvara has ordered 47 Volvo 8900 Electric buses for deployment in regional transport across Skåne and urban transport services in Helsingborg. The order reflects growing adoption of battery-electric buses in Scandinavian public transportation networks.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Vietnam: VinFast unveils second-generation VF 8
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Vietnamese automaker VinFast has unveiled the second-generation VF 8 with a comprehensive redesign, planning to introduce the model first in its domestic market. The updated vehicle represents the company's continued evolution in the competitive Southeast Asian EV segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: XPENG GX Popularity Jacks Up XPENG Orders To ~50,000 Vehicles In May
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese EV manufacturer XPeng's new GX model has generated approximately 50,000 vehicle orders in May 2026, significantly boosting the company's sales momentum. Deutsche Bank analysts project the popular model will substantially elevate XPeng's market position within China's competitive electric vehicle landscape.

science-space.title: NASA to Announce Artemis III Crew, Provide Mission Progress Update
science-space.summary: NASA will announce the astronauts assigned to the Artemis III mission and provide a comprehensive progress update during a live event on June 9 at Johnson Space Center in Houston. The announcement represents a major milestone in humanity's return to the Moon and will be streamed on NASA+ and YouTube.

science-space.title: NASA Provides Update on Moon Base Rovers, Landers, Missions
science-space.summary: NASA announced new contracts for lunar rovers and uncrewed cargo landers as part of its Moon Base infrastructure development, sharing target launch timeframes and milestones for exploration missions to the lunar South Pole. The agency is advancing critical hardware and logistics capabilities to support sustained human lunar exploration.

science-space.title: Artemis moon base will cover 'hundreds of square miles' with hopping drones and new lunar rovers, NASA says
science-space.summary: NASA has awarded $1 billion in contracts to develop a lunar base covering hundreds of square miles, featuring hopping scout drones and advanced rovers to mark the facility's perimeter. This expansion of lunar infrastructure represents a significant step toward establishing a permanent human presence on the Moon.

science-space.title: Jaclyn Kagey Shapes Humanity's Return to the Moon
science-space.summary: As NASA's Artemis extravehicular activity lead, Jaclyn Kagey plays a central role in preparing astronauts to safely explore the lunar surface, particularly the Moon's South Pole region that has never been visited by humans. Her work is essential to establishing protocols and training for future deep space exploration missions.

science-space.title: 'Very interesting wiggles' in data from silent NASA Mars spacecraft lead to unexpected solar wind discovery
science-space.summary: Data from NASA's MAVEN spacecraft revealed a previously unknown atmospheric effect on Mars, demonstrating how solar storms shape planets without strong magnetic fields. This discovery provides new insights into planetary atmospheric dynamics and the effects of solar wind on celestial bodies.

science-space.title: Unusual red northern lights over Japan suggest some solar storms are stronger than we thought
science-space.summary: Analysis of rare crimson auroras observed over Japan revealed that geomagnetic storm effects extend hundreds of miles higher into Earth's atmosphere than previously expected. The findings challenge existing models of solar storm strength and atmospheric response to space weather events.

science-space.title: New Instrument Used Antarctic Ice Sheet to Probe Extreme Universe
science-space.summary: The Payload for Ultrahigh Energy Observations (PUEO), a NASA Astrophysics Pioneers Program mission, uses the Antarctic ice sheet as a detection medium to study the most energetic particles in the universe. This innovative approach leverages natural resources to advance fundamental physics research.

science-space.title: Could a cosmic uncertainty principle help explain dark matter?
science-space.summary: Researchers propose that the universe may operate under its own version of Heisenberg's uncertainty principle, potentially explaining dark energy without requiring new physics. This theoretical framework offers a novel approach to understanding one of cosmology's greatest mysteries.

science-space.title: Satellites imaged an underwater volcano erupting — but scientists have no idea what's actually happening on the seafloor
science-space.summary: Satellite imagery captured footage of an underwater volcano eruption in a remote oceanic region that remains largely unexplored and mysterious to scientists. The discovery highlights significant gaps in human understanding of deep-sea geological activity and the need for enhanced ocean floor monitoring.

science-space.title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Students in New York
science-space.summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir will answer prerecorded STEM questions from New York students during a live Earth-to-space call from the International Space Station on May 28. The educational event aims to inspire student interest in science and space exploration through direct interaction with active space station crew members.

finance-markets.title: Memory Chip Frenzy Sends SK Hynix, Micron Into $1 Trillion Club
finance-markets.summary: SK Hynix and Micron Technology have each surpassed $1 trillion in market capitalization for the first time, driven by a sharp rally in memory-chip stocks. The surge reflects investor confidence that sustained artificial intelligence demand will drive a revaluation of the semiconductor industry.

finance-markets.title: Asian Stocks Hit Record on Chip Rally, Oil Drops: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Asian equity markets reached record levels, propelled by renewed momentum in technology stocks and easing geopolitical tensions. Oil prices declined while the dollar weakened amid improving sentiment.

finance-markets.title: Samsung Union Votes in Favor of Deal, Averting Chip Plant Strike
finance-markets.summary: Samsung Electronics' largest union approved a compensation agreement providing chip workers with an average bonus of approximately $340,000, averting a strike that posed risks to global semiconductor supply chains. The deal represents a significant labor settlement for the critical technology sector.

finance-markets.title: UK imposes sanctions on crypto exchange tied to billionaire Justin Sun
finance-markets.summary: The UK Foreign Office announced sanctions against Huobi and other entities, citing their role in helping Russia circumvent economic penalties. The action signals increased regulatory focus on cryptocurrency platforms facilitating sanctions evasion.

finance-markets.title: Oil Dips as US Sees Progress in Iran Deal Despite Fresh Tensions
finance-markets.summary: Oil prices edged lower on optimism that the United States and Iran will reach a peace agreement despite ongoing hostilities and uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz. Market sentiment has improved regarding the resolution of the three-month conflict.

finance-markets.title: BP chair's departure turns spotlight on Meg O'Neill to deliver swift turnaround
finance-markets.summary: BP's board chair Albert Manifold has been removed amid concerns over his conduct and governance practices, including improper use of personal devices, placing pressure on successor Meg O'Neill to execute a rapid corporate turnaround. The leadership transition comes during a critical period for the energy major's strategic direction.

finance-markets.title: EU Firms Warm to China Even as Tensions Spiral Over Export Surge
finance-markets.summary: A new survey reveals European companies operating in China are growing more optimistic about their business prospects, even as the European Union prepares to address an expanding trade imbalance with Beijing. The findings highlight disconnects between corporate sentiment and governmental trade concerns.

finance-markets.title: UBS's Khan Says AI Will Have 'Ramifications' on Jobs
finance-markets.summary: UBS Group's Asia Pacific president Iqbal Khan cautioned that artificial intelligence will impact employment levels despite improving productivity and freeing up organizational capacity. The remarks highlight ongoing concerns about AI-driven labor market disruptions across the financial sector.

finance-markets.title: Insurance Mogul Lindberg Gets 12 Years for $2 Billion Fraud
finance-markets.summary: Greg Lindberg, founder of a North Carolina investment firm, was sentenced to 12 years in prison for defrauding insurance policyholders of over $2 billion in reserves, using proceeds to fund a lavish lifestyle including jets, mansions, and a yacht. The conviction underscores regulatory enforcement against large-scale financial fraud.

finance-markets.title: Nine countries pull out of Ukraine ammunition coalition
finance-markets.summary: The Czech-led initiative to supply Ukraine with millions of artillery shells has lost half its participating countries since December, according to the coalition's president. The departure of nine nations signals waning multilateral commitment to sustained military support for Kyiv.

tech-ai.title: UK Visa Portal spilled thousands of applicants' passports and selfies online — and hasn't fixed the leak
tech-ai.summary: A third-party website used for UK visa applications exposed sensitive documents including passports and selfies belonging to thousands of applicants. Rather than addressing the security breach, the company responded with legal threats instead of remediation efforts.

tech-ai.title: DuckDuckGo installs are up 30% as users reject being 'force-fed' Google's AI Search
tech-ai.summary: Google's overhaul of its Search product at I/O 2026, which replaced traditional blue links with AI agents, has triggered significant user backlash. The negative reaction has driven a 30 percent spike in DuckDuckGo app installations as users seek alternative search options.

tech-ai.title: Dutch government blocks US company from acquisition, citing 'risk to public interest'
tech-ai.summary: The Dutch government has blocked a US company's acquisition of a cloud infrastructure provider that hosts the nation's digital ID service. The decision reflects Europe's broader efforts to reduce technological dependence on American companies for critical infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
tech-ai.summary: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B funding round led by CapitalG, more than doubling its valuation to $1.3 billion in just one year. The company's 5x growth in usage over six months demonstrates market validation for the multi-AI-model platform approach.

tech-ai.title: SpaceX's Starlink nabs American Airlines contract, another win for its IPO
tech-ai.summary: SpaceX announced that American Airlines plans to install Starlink satellite internet on over 500 aircraft, representing a significant commercial win for the company ahead of its IPO. The airline partnership demonstrates growing adoption of satellite-based connectivity for in-flight services.

tech-ai.title: Trump administration wants nuclear startups to use plutonium for their reactors
tech-ai.summary: The U.S. government is encouraging nuclear technology startups to develop uses for the dozens of tons of weapons-grade plutonium currently in government stockpiles. The initiative aims to repurpose legacy nuclear materials for civilian energy applications.

tech-ai.title: This startup is betting India's gig economy can train the world's robots
tech-ai.summary: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is employing gig workers in India to collect robotics and AI training data by wearing camera-equipped caps and sensor devices. The approach leverages India's gig economy to address the critical data acquisition challenge facing AI and robotics development.

tech-ai.title: Starship's path to reusability looks murky after SpaceX's S-1
tech-ai.summary: SpaceX's recent IPO filing and latest Starship rocket test flight provide insight into the company's realistic near-term trajectory for achieving rocket reusability. The data suggests a development timeline that may disappoint both SpaceX optimists and critics.

tech-ai.title: Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI?
tech-ai.summary: Analysis by researcher Linch Zhang suggests that AI may have been used to write significant portions of Pope Leo XIV's latest encyclical on artificial intelligence, with some paragraphs scoring 40 to 100 percent AI-generated according to the Pangram detector. The document exhibits linguistic patterns consistent with AI-generated text, including excessive use of terms common in Claude's outputs.

tech-ai.title: Trump administration permits Volvo to keep selling connected cars in the US
tech-ai.summary: The Trump administration has approved Volvo's continued operation in the U.S. market, allowing the Chinese-owned automaker (majority held by Geely Holdings) to proceed with expansion plans for its American manufacturing facility. The decision enables Volvo to move forward with its connected vehicle strategy in the United States.

world-news.title: Dozens killed in Lebanon as Israel intensifies strikes
world-news.summary: Israel conducted strikes against over 100 Hezbollah infrastructure sites and fighters in Lebanon, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowing to 'crush' the militant organization. The escalation marks a significant intensification of military operations in the region.

world-news.title: Iran condemns US strikes as 'gross violation' of ceasefire
world-news.summary: Iran has denounced US military strikes as a violation of ceasefire agreements, with the condemnation occurring during ongoing peace negotiations between Iranian and Qatari diplomats in Doha. The dispute threatens to derail diplomatic efforts to resolve regional tensions.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Israel kills 31 in Lebanon; Tehran blasts US truce violation
world-news.summary: Israeli military operations in Lebanon have killed 3,213 people and injured 9,737 since March 2, according to Lebanon's Health Ministry, while Iran continues to criticize alleged US violations of ceasefire agreements. The ongoing conflict represents a major regional escalation affecting multiple nations.

world-news.title: Can EU find a Russia whisperer to mediate an end to war in Ukraine?
world-news.summary: The European Union is seeking a mediator to facilitate peace negotiations between Russia and Ukraine following the US withdrawal from trilateral talks. The diplomatic initiative reflects growing European efforts to shape the resolution of the Ukraine conflict.

world-news.title: UN chief 'deeply concerned' by Moscow plan for Kyiv strikes
world-news.summary: UN Secretary-General António Guterres expressed serious concern after Russia announced plans to conduct strikes against Kyiv, signaling renewed escalation in the Ukraine conflict. The statement reflects international anxiety over Russia's military intentions.

world-news.title: Russia 'relentlessly targeting' critical infrastructure and democracy, GCHQ says
world-news.summary: The UK's GCHQ intelligence agency has warned that Russia is conducting systematic attacks on critical infrastructure and democratic systems, with the agency's leadership planning to outline defensive measures. The assessment underscores Russia's ongoing information and infrastructure warfare against Western nations.

world-news.title: Hundreds of children die within months as measles cases soar in Bangladesh
world-news.summary: Bangladesh has recorded over 60,000 suspected measles cases in just two months, resulting in hundreds of pediatric deaths and representing a significant public health crisis. The outbreak highlights global disease surveillance concerns and regional healthcare capacity challenges.

world-news.title: How decision-making happens in Iran
world-news.summary: Analysis of Iran's decision-making structure reveals that negotiators operate under mandates established by the Supreme National Security Council and approved by the Supreme Leader. Understanding this governance hierarchy is essential for assessing Iranian policy positions in international negotiations.

world-news.title: Three killed and dozens injured in Israeli strike on Gaza City, medics and witnesses say
world-news.summary: An Israeli military strike on Gaza City killed three people and injured dozens, targeting a Hamas commander whose predecessor was killed in a similar operation in May. The strike continues the pattern of targeted operations against Hamas leadership in the Palestinian territory.

world-news.title: Group of women and children with alleged ISIL ties returns to Australia
world-news.summary: A group of women and children with alleged connections to ISIS has returned to Australia, prompting investigation by the Australian Federal Police, though no arrests have been made at this time. The case reflects ongoing security concerns regarding the return of foreign fighters and their families from conflict zones.

crypto-web3.title: Trump praises prediction markets, defends CFTC as court cases compound
crypto-web3.summary: President Trump publicly backed CFTC Chair Michael Selig's authority over prediction markets, calling it "critically important" that the agency maintain exclusive regulatory control. The statement comes as legal challenges to prediction market regulation intensify across multiple jurisdictions.

crypto-web3.title: UK sanctions Huobi and ruble stablecoin issuer in crackdown on Russia crypto networks
crypto-web3.summary: The United Kingdom applied banking-style sanctions to crypto exchanges for the first time, requiring U.K. financial firms to freeze funds and trace transactions related to Russia. The action marks a significant escalation in regulatory oversight of cryptocurrency infrastructure used to circumvent international sanctions.

crypto-web3.title: Spain blocks Polymarket, Kalshi for operating without licenses amid widening global crackdown on prediction markets
crypto-web3.summary: Spain has blocked major prediction market platforms Polymarket and Kalshi for operating without proper licenses, reflecting a growing international regulatory crackdown on the sector. The action demonstrates divergent global approaches to prediction market oversight despite U.S. policy support.

crypto-web3.title: DeFi TVL slides 14% since KelpDAO exploit as risk appetite retreats
crypto-web3.summary: Decentralized finance total value locked has declined 14 percent following the KelpDAO security breach, signaling reduced investor risk appetite in the sector. The exploit exposed critical infrastructure vulnerabilities that are prompting broader reassessment of DeFi protocol safety.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase's Base launches AI tool for ChatGPT to manage crypto wallets and DeFi apps
crypto-web3.summary: Coinbase's Base blockchain has launched an integration connecting user wallets to AI assistants including ChatGPT and Claude through the Model Context Protocol, enabling natural language management of crypto assets and DeFi interactions. The development represents a significant step in bridging AI systems with blockchain applications for mainstream adoption.

crypto-web3.title: Joe Lubin-backed Ethereum treasury firm SharpLink to join the Russell indexes
crypto-web3.summary: Ethereum treasury management firm SharpLink, backed by Consensys founder Joe Lubin, will be included in the Russell indexes, potentially attracting significant passive investment flows. The addition comes despite the company's stock declining 95 percent from its peak over the past year.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto industry defends OCC charters for Ripple, Coinbase and others after Sen. Warren calls them unlawful
crypto-web3.summary: The Digital Chamber rejected Senator Elizabeth Warren's accusations that crypto firms improperly received national trust charters from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency. The dispute reflects ongoing debate over appropriate regulatory frameworks for cryptocurrency-focused financial institutions.

crypto-web3.title: TeraWulf jumps 13% on AI data center expansion in Kentucky
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf announced plans for a new 1-gigawatt data center facility in Kentucky to service artificial intelligence workloads, driving a 13 percent stock increase. The expansion reflects the company's strategic pivot toward high-performance computing infrastructure, with HPC revenue now exceeding bitcoin mining operations.

crypto-web3.title: Bermuda, the small island nation with huge crypto ambitions
crypto-web3.summary: Bermuda announced plans to become the world's first fully onchain economy, a strategic initiative government officials believe will generate new economic opportunities for the island nation. The move positions Bermuda as a pioneering jurisdiction for blockchain-based financial and governmental infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Solana privacy layer Umbra launches confidential vesting with Streamflow targeting $97B token unlock market
crypto-web3.summary: Solana's privacy protocol Umbra and token distribution platform Streamflow have launched confidential vesting functionality enabling projects to distribute tokens privately at scale. The integration targets the $97 billion token unlock market, addressing privacy concerns around large-scale token releases.

health-biotech.title: Lilly reports new data on PCSK9 base editing gene therapy from Verve buyout
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly announced positive Phase 1b trial results for a base editing gene therapy acquired through its Verve Therapeutics purchase, demonstrating efficacy in reducing cholesterol levels. The company plans to advance the therapy to mid-stage testing by year-end, marking significant progress in genetic approaches to cardiovascular disease.

health-biotech.title: Pharmalittle: We're reading about a Lilly gene therapy for cholesterol, three new Lilly deals, and more
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly reported that a high-dose gene-editing therapy reduced cholesterol levels by 62 percent in Phase 1 clinical trial participants, representing a major advancement in genetic medicine for metabolic disorders. The announcement reflects the company's aggressive expansion in gene therapy development.

health-biotech.title: Lilly to acquire three vaccine biotechs for up to $3.8B
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly is acquiring three vaccine developers in a strategic consolidation move valued at up to $3.8 billion, significantly expanding its infectious disease portfolio and strengthening its position in vaccine development. The acquisitions represent the company's eighth and ninth deals as it deploys capital from its blockbuster GLP-1 franchise.

health-biotech.title: Outlook says FDA will sign off on its version of Avastin for wet AMD
health-biotech.summary: Outlook Therapeutics announced FDA approval of its reformulated version of Avastin for wet age-related macular degeneration after three prior rejections dating back to 2023. The clearance represents a significant regulatory victory for the company's ocular therapeutic candidate.

health-biotech.title: Kardigan readies Nasdaq IPO to back three cardio drugs
health-biotech.summary: Kardigan, a clinical-stage cardiovascular biotech based in Princeton, New Jersey, has filed for a Nasdaq IPO to fund development of three heart health drug candidates. The filing reflects continued investor appetite for early-stage drug developers in the cardiovascular therapeutic space.

health-biotech.title: Can AI do scientific research? Billions chase hopes of superintelligence
health-biotech.summary: Researchers at Lila Sciences and other organizations are investing heavily in AI models designed to accelerate drug discovery, including applications for mRNA molecule selection and protein expression optimization. The article examines whether artificial intelligence can meaningfully contribute to fundamental scientific research and drug development.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: $775 billion, $1.2 billion, and $38k
health-biotech.summary: Medicaid cuts resulting from Republicans' 2025 tax law are projected to exceed previous forecasts, with significant implications for healthcare access and coverage across the United States. The funding reductions present substantial challenges for state-run health insurance programs and vulnerable populations.

health-biotech.title: Astellas' five-year plan includes chasing record sales and cost cuts
health-biotech.summary: Japanese pharmaceutical company Astellas Pharma announced a five-year strategic plan targeting doubled sales for five key medicines by fiscal year 2030, including the Pfizer-partnered bladder cancer drug Padcev. The strategy combines aggressive revenue growth targets with operational cost reduction initiatives.

health-biotech.title: Cartesian secures funding to 'accelerate' launch; Editas sells $125M in stock
health-biotech.summary: Cartesian Therapeutics secured $150 million in financing, including an initial $50 million tranche from K2 HealthVentures, to accelerate its commercial product launch, while gene-editing company Editas divested $125 million in equity. Both moves reflect continued investor confidence in emerging biotech platforms despite market volatility.

health-biotech.title: What do early data from Utah's Doctronic AI pilot show?
health-biotech.summary: Early data from a Utah pilot program utilizing AI technology to streamline prescription renewals provide initial evidence of artificial intelligence applications in healthcare operations. The pilot also includes updates on health tech companies such as Oura preparing public market debuts.

startups-vc.title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
startups-vc.summary: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B round led by CapitalG, more than doubling its valuation to $1.3 billion within a year. The funding reflects the company's 5x usage growth over six months as enterprises increasingly adopt multi-AI-model infrastructure.

startups-vc.title: Digital Banking Startup Mercury Lands $200M At $5.2B Valuation Amid Fintech Funding Uptick
startups-vc.summary: Digital banking platform Mercury closed a $200 million Series D round at a $5.2 billion valuation, representing a 49% increase from its previous $3.5 billion valuation achieved in March 2025. The funding demonstrates continued investor confidence in the fintech sector's growth trajectory.

startups-vc.title: Amazon fulfillment competitor Stord raises $250M at $3B valuation
startups-vc.summary: Stord, a logistics platform offering warehouse networks and inventory management software for e-commerce brands, raised $250 million at a $3 billion valuation. The funding positions the company as an alternative to Amazon's fulfillment services, enabling brands to maintain customer relationships while competing on speed and scale.

startups-vc.title: Embodied AI Fuels Record Robotics Funding In China As IPO Momentum Builds
startups-vc.summary: China-based robotics companies have already raised $5.6 billion across 176 deals through mid-May 2026, matching the entire year's funding from 2021. The surge in embodied AI investment underscores China's dominance in robotics development and signals growing IPO momentum in the sector.

startups-vc.title: SolarSquare in talks to raise up to $60M as India's rooftop solar market draws major VC interest
startups-vc.summary: SolarSquare is in advanced discussions to raise up to $60 million in funding, with an expected valuation reaching $500 million upon closing next month. The round reflects increased venture capital attention to India's rooftop solar market and distributed renewable energy solutions.

startups-vc.title: In Charts: Seed Deals Keep Getting Bigger As Odds Of Reaching Series A Fall Dramatically
startups-vc.summary: Seed-stage funding has increased significantly, with some startups now raising $8 million to $10 million in early rounds traditionally associated with later stages. However, the path to Series A funding has become increasingly difficult, creating a more challenging venture landscape for emerging companies.

startups-vc.title: Peec, one of Berlin's rising startups, more than doubled annualized revenue in months to $10M, sources say
startups-vc.summary: Peec, a platform helping brands track their presence in AI-generated search results, has more than doubled its annualized revenue to $10 million in recent months. The startup's rapid growth exemplifies the emerging European trend of AI-focused SaaS companies addressing new market needs.

startups-vc.title: After A 6-Figure Fertility Journey, This Founder Built An IVF Startup With 'Outcome Protection'
startups-vc.summary: Gaia, an IVF-focused healthcare startup, leverages artificial intelligence and machine learning trained on millions of anonymized fertility outcomes to assess treatment risk and probability for patients. The company addresses a significant gap in fertility treatment optimization informed by data-driven insights.

startups-vc.title: The IPO Comeback Has A Catch
startups-vc.summary: While IPO activity is rebounding, the threshold for going public has steadily increased, leaving many mid-sized companies and their shareholders without viable exit opportunities. This trend is creating pressure for a more mature private secondary market to address the liquidity gap for maturing private companies.

startups-vc.title: How Owner.com's CRO Is Closing $2M+ in ARR Per Rep With AI: 5 Things You Can Steal
startups-vc.summary: Owner.com, a vertical AI platform for independent restaurants, is achieving exceptional sales productivity of $2 million-plus in ARR per representative by combining AI capabilities with vertical market focus. The company's success demonstrates how specialized AI solutions can drive outsized financial results for niche B2B markets.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A cybercrime group has launched a data extortion attack against Canvas, a widely-used education technology platform affecting nearly 9,000 educational institutions across the United States. The attackers defaced the platform's login page with ransom demands threatening to leak data from 275 million students and faculty members.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Patches SharePoint RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45659 Across Server Versions
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft has released security updates to address a remote code execution vulnerability in SharePoint tracked as CVE-2026-45659 with a CVSS severity score of 8.8. The flaw allows attackers to exploit deserialization of untrusted data without requiring specialized conditions for successful exploitation.

cybersecurity.title: Feeding Frenzy: 'Megalodon' Malware Infects Thousands of GitHub Repos
cybersecurity.summary: A malicious campaign injected thousands of compromised commits into over 5,500 GitHub repositories within a six-hour window, stealing developer credentials and secrets. The widespread supply chain attack demonstrates the vulnerability of open-source development infrastructure to coordinated malware distribution.

cybersecurity.title: MFA Prompt Bombing: Why Your Second Factor Isn't Saving You
cybersecurity.summary: Attackers have developed techniques to bypass multi-factor authentication by convincing users to voluntarily provide their second-factor codes through prompt bombing attacks rather than stealing them. This emerging threat undermines organizations' reliance on MFA as a primary defense against account compromise.

cybersecurity.title: MuddyWater Uses DLL Side-Loading in Espionage Campaign Targeting 9 Countries
cybersecurity.summary: The Iranian-linked threat group MuddyWater has been attributed to a new espionage campaign affecting at least nine organizations across multiple continents in early 2026. The campaign targeted diverse sectors including manufacturing, education, finance, and government agencies using DLL side-loading techniques.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle have released record volumes of security patches this month, with AI platforms increasingly effective at identifying vulnerabilities in human-written code. The rapid pace of patches reflects both the growing exploitation of discovered flaws and vendors' accelerated remediation efforts.

cybersecurity.title: KnowledgeDeliver LMS Flaw Exploited to Deploy Godzilla and Cobalt Strike
cybersecurity.summary: A high-severity vulnerability in the KnowledgeDeliver learning management system was exploited as a zero-day to deploy the Godzilla web shell and Cobalt Strike Beacon malware. The CVE-2026-5426 flaw stems from hard-coded ASP.NET machine keys that enabled attackers to compromise affected systems in Japan.

cybersecurity.title: CERT-In Recommends 12-Hour Patching for Internet-Facing Flaws Amid AI-Assisted Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: India's Computer Emergency Response Team has issued new mandatory guidelines requiring organizations to patch critical internet-exposed vulnerabilities within 12 hours due to threat actors' increasing use of artificial intelligence and large language models. The accelerated patching timeline reflects the heightened risk from automated vulnerability exploitation.

cybersecurity.title: Iranian Hackers Deploy MiniFast and MiniJunk V2 via Phishing and SEO Poisoning
cybersecurity.summary: The Iranian state-sponsored threat actor Nimbus Manticore has launched a new campaign using phishing and SEO poisoning to distribute MiniFast and MiniJunk V2 malware targeting the aviation and software sectors. The activity follows the joint U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran in late February 2026 and affects targets across the U.S., Europe, and Middle East.

cybersecurity.title: China's Webworm Uses Discord, Microsoft Graphs to Hack EU Governments
cybersecurity.summary: An advanced persistent threat group has been linked to intrusions against European government entities using Discord and Microsoft Graph API for command and control alongside SOCKS proxies such as SoftEther VPN. The sophisticated campaign demonstrates threat actors' abuse of legitimate cloud services for covert communications infrastructure.

energy-climate.title: New coal plants hit '10-year' global high in 2025 – but power output still fell
energy-climate.summary: Global coal-fired power plant construction reached a decade-high in 2025, signaling continued investment in fossil fuel infrastructure despite climate commitments. However, the overall power output from coal plants declined, reflecting growing competition from renewable energy sources and shifting energy markets.

energy-climate.title: US energy storage installations hit Q1 record, up 32% year over year: SEIA
energy-climate.summary: United States energy storage deployments reached record levels in the first quarter of 2026, with a 32% year-over-year increase driven primarily by robust data center demand. The clean energy trade group projects 613 GWh of cumulative deployment by 2030, though federal policy uncertainty poses risks to sustained growth.

energy-climate.title: CAISO recommends 38 transmission projects costing around $6.7B
energy-climate.summary: California's grid operator has recommended 38 transmission infrastructure projects valued at approximately $6.7 billion, representing a strategic shift in grid planning toward managing load growth alongside renewable integration. More than half of the projects address forecasted demand increases, marking an evolution from a renewable-access-focused approach to balanced capacity planning.

energy-climate.title: Seven countermeasures against negative electricity prices
energy-climate.summary: Germany has experienced widespread negative electricity prices due to excess solar generation during low-demand periods, prompting industry calls for immediate infrastructure and policy interventions. The bne industry association urges accelerated energy storage deployment, smarter EV charging, rapid smart-meter rollout, and improved grid flexibility to better manage renewable surpluses.

energy-climate.title: New Mexico regulators approve SPS' $9B, gas-heavy resource plan
energy-climate.summary: New Mexico regulators have approved a $9 billion energy resource portfolio that relies heavily on natural gas, including 2,088 MW of new gas capacity alongside renewable components. The plan includes 1,100 MW of wind, 189 MW of solar, and 472 MW of battery storage, reflecting continued reliance on fossil fuels in the state's energy transition.

energy-climate.title: Storing renewables with compressed air in urban heating pipeline
energy-climate.summary: Chinese researchers have developed a novel heating pipeline compressed-air energy storage system that repurposes existing district heating networks to store surplus renewable electricity and thermal energy. This innovative approach delivers improved efficiency and lower infrastructure costs compared to conventional tank-based compressed air storage systems.

energy-climate.title: Australia seeks 5 GW renewable generation in new tender
energy-climate.summary: Australia has launched a competitive tender through its Capacity Investment Scheme seeking 5 GW of new renewable energy generation capacity in the National Electricity Market. This development represents continued government support for accelerating renewable deployment as part of the country's energy transition.

energy-climate.title: Study finds POLO back junction solar cells could outperform PERC on costs in Europe
energy-climate.summary: German researchers have determined that POLO back-junction solar cells offer significant cost and efficiency advantages over PERC technology for European manufacturing, including reduced silver use and lower levelized electricity costs. The findings position POLO technology as a competitive option for scaling competitive solar manufacturing capacity in Europe.

energy-climate.title: Saudi Arabia gets first BESS manufacturing facility
energy-climate.summary: China's ZOE Energy Storage will establish the first battery energy storage system manufacturing facility in Saudi Arabia, scaling production capacity to 18 GWh. This development signals growing regional investment in energy storage infrastructure amid expanding renewable deployment across the Middle East.

energy-climate.title: Türkiye reduces grid fee for around 800 unlicensed solar plants
energy-climate.summary: Türkiye's energy regulator has implemented a 68 percent reduction in grid fees for approximately 800 unlicensed solar installations that have completed their ten-year purchase guarantee periods. This regulatory adjustment aims to improve the economic viability of distributed solar generation as initial subsidy periods expire.

asia-pacific.title: South Korea’s lonely, stressed Gen Z find comfort in apps that do nothing
asia-pacific.summary:
At 2am, Kim, a 25-year-old office worker, opens a site designed to look like a food delivery app, though he has no plan to order. He chooses menu items, drops them into a cart and simulates the experience of placing an order.
“It somehow feels like I actually ordered something,” he said.
Kim said the habit helps him resist late-night cravings.
“There are many times when I crave food late at night but hold back to save money. It feels like a real delivery app, so I somehow keep looking at it,”...

asia-pacific.title: Beyond visitor numbers: Hong Kong must track the full worth of its art and culture
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong’s M+ museum recently signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with the Centre Pompidou in France, cementing a multi-year partnership that will cover collection, research and talent exchanges. This follows the 12 MOUs signed by the West Kowloon Cultural District Authority during this year’s International Cultural Summit – bringing its total international partnerships to 46.
With billions of dollars being poured into mega-events, arts hubs and wider cultural initiatives, we are...

asia-pacific.title: Trump-endorsed Paxton wins Texas primary run-off, ousting veteran senator
asia-pacific.summary:
Texas Attorney General Ken ⁠Paxton ousted four-term US Senator John Cornyn on Tuesday, riding President Donald ⁠Trump’s endorsement to a run-off victory for the Republican Senate nomination, US media projected.
The race was called shortly after polls closed in Texas’ westernmost counties, which are in a separate time zone from most of the state.
Paxton’s victory will force Cornyn into retirement early next year – and the Republican political establishment in Washington into embracing a candidate

asia-pacific.title: EU firms in China starting to feel less gloomy as Beijing offers stability: report
asia-pacific.summary:
European companies’ confidence in the Chinese market is showing signs of rebounding for the first time since 2022, as Beijing is increasingly perceived as a “champion” of stability amid rising global volatility, according to a survey released on Wednesday.
After years of spiralling pessimism, the European Union Chamber of Commerce in China found that “the intensity of the deterioration in confidence in China’s business environment has eased” among its members over the past year.
In the group’s..

asia-pacific.title: Chinese man who suffered painful urination for 3 years discovers 1.3kg stone in bladder
asia-pacific.summary:
A Chinese man who endured three years of intense pain while urinating finally went to the hospital and discovered he had a 1.3kg stone in his bladder.
The man, 56, from southern China’s Guangdong province, surnamed Chen, recently went to the Xuwen Hospital, Affiliated Hospital of Guangdong Medical University, to have his bladder checked.
Chen had experienced frequent and painful urination for three years. The symptoms had become so serious recently that he dared not travel far during the day and

asia-pacific.title: How Hong Kong’s first astronaut triggered a space education race
asia-pacific.summary:
Lai Ka-ying’s selection as Hong Kong’s first astronaut has sparked a more than threefold surge in aerospace and satellite-design course inquiries at a learning centre, while industry leaders have called for sites to be refitted as immersive education facilities.
Lai, a police superintendent who has also become China’s first female payload specialist, made her historic journey into space on Sunday with the launch of the Shenzhou-23 mission.
Jeffrey Ho, CEO of Star Club Scientist Education, said..

asia-pacific.title: Insilico teams up with US firm to build AI models predicting disease decades ahead
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong-listed artificial intelligence drug-discovery company Insilico Medicine has struck a collaboration with US-headquartered Human Longevity to co-develop what they describe as “the industry’s first large-scale” AI foundation models capable of predicting diseases – such as cancer – decades before clinical symptoms appear.
The partnership was announced as the global longevity market, fuelled by an ageing population and rising consumption demand, was valued at about US$5.3 trillion and...

asia-pacific.title: Passengers praise efficiency as Hong Kong airport’s Terminal 2 opens
asia-pacific.summary:
Passengers have praised the expanded second terminal at Hong Kong airport for its efficiency upon reopening, but say more convenient boarding arrangements are still needed.
Hong Kong Airlines became the first – and so far only – carrier to use the upgraded terminal, which will accommodate 15 carriers moving in between Wednesday and June 10.
By 6am, hundreds of travellers were checking in and dropping off baggage at the terminal, with no long queues observed.
“I was able to complete baggage drop.

asia-pacific.title: Can Japan lead push to halt space militarisation as nuclear rivalry heats up?
asia-pacific.summary:
The collapse of UN talks on the future of the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty has sharpened concern in Japan that outer space could become the next arena for nuclear and military rivalry.
With Russia accused of developing a nuclear anti-satellite capability, China and Russia advancing weapons that can disable satellites, and the US pursuing space-based missile defence systems, some in Tokyo say Japan should take the lead in drawing up new rules for the peaceful use of space.
Analysts say any...

asia-pacific.title: Nasa lays out moon base plans with landers, buggies and drones at the top of the list
asia-pacific.summary:
Nasa is already ordering landers, rovers and drones for a sprawling moon base, less than two months after the Artemis II’s record-breaking lunar fly-around.
The space agency outlined the first phase of its moon base plans on Tuesday, awarding hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts to four US companies.
Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin will provide a pair of landers to deliver moon buggies to the lunar surface, at a spot near the moon’s south pole.
These so-called lunar terrain vehicles will be...
